messages.content: Why did the sun and moon series have a different artstyle from the xy or thd black and white series

The decision to change the artstyle for the Sun and Moon series was likely made by the creators and producers of the show in order to differentiate it from previous series and give it a fresh, updated look. Additionally, each Pokémon series has its own unique setting and storyline, so the artstyle may have been chosen to better fit the themes and tone of the Sun and Moon storyline. Ultimately, the change in artstyle may have been made to attract new viewers and keep the show feeling current and innovative.
Why was thr xy anime artstyle the same as black and white

The decision to keep the artstyle consistent between the XY and the Black and White series may have been to maintain a sense of continuity and familiarity for the audience. By keeping the artstyle consistent, viewers can easily transition between the two series without feeling a jarring change in visual style.

Additionally, maintaining the same artstyle for multiple series may have also been a strategic decision to appeal to the existing fanbase and maintain their interest in the show. Overall, the decision to keep the artstyle the same between the XY and Black and White series may have been a way to provide a sense of cohesion and consistency in the Pokémon anime universe.
Why did Ash get brown irises in the black and white series

The change in Ash's eye color from black (or very dark brown) to brown irises in the Black and White series of Pokémon may have been a creative decision by the animation team to give his character a slightly different look. 

Though the exact reason for this change is not officially confirmed, it is not uncommon for animated characters to undergo slight visual changes over time to keep the character design fresh and interesting. The change in Ash's eye color may have been a way to subtly update his appearance for the new series while still maintaining the essence of his character.

Ultimately, changes in character design, such as Ash's eye color, are often made to add variety and visual interest to a long-running series like Pokémon.
Why the xy series ash design make him look older

The XY series of Pokémon introduced a slightly updated character design for Ash that gave him a more mature appearance compared to previous series. There are a few possible reasons for this change:

1. Character development: As the Pokémon anime has progressed over the years, Ash's character has matured and grown through his experiences and adventures. The updated design in the XY series may have been a reflection of Ash's personal growth and development as a Pokémon trainer.

2. Target audience: The XY series may have been intended to appeal to a slightly older demographic of viewers, so giving Ash a more mature appearance could have been a way to connect with this audience and make the show feel more relatable to older viewers.

3. Artistic choice: The animation team may have simply wanted to refresh Ash's character design and experiment with different styles to keep the series visually engaging and interesting for viewers.

Overall, the decision to make Ash look older in the XY series was likely a combination of character development, target audience considerations, and artistic choices.
When did the writers write decalore island arc in best wishes

The Decalore Island arc in the Pokémon Best Wishes series (Black & White series in English) was written and aired as part of the series in Japan. 

It aired from October 17, 2013, to October 30, 2014, and consisted of several episodes that focused on Ash and his friends exploring the Decalore Islands and encountering new Pokémon and challenges along the way.

The writers and creators of the Pokémon anime series typically plan out the story arcs and episodes well in advance, so the Decalore Island arc would have been written and developed as part of the overall storyline for the Best Wishes series.
